speaker: Mr. Swine
description: Voice-reference dialogue samples for Mr. Swine in Pig & Swine RPG. These are draft samples, not final committed game text.

voice_profile.core_voice: charming, evasive, affectionate, reckless, allergic to linear explanation, eventually sincere
comedy_engine: uses polished phrasing to avoid simple admissions
treats suspicious documents as unfortunate genres
answers factual questions sideways until pinned down
do: make him funny without making him stupid
let him hide discomfort behind charm
give him useful factual memory in Chapter 4
show affection for Pig & Swine underneath evasiveness
do_not: make Japan or translation officers the joke
let him win because he is silly
turn every line into pure nonsense

relationship_dynamics.Mr. Pig: affectionate, exasperating, brotherly/founder tension; Pig panics, Swine deflects
relationship_dynamics.Mr. Murrow: fears Murrow's audits and questions; respects his competence
relationship_dynamics.Crab: Crab sees through him; Swine performs innocence anyway
relationship_dynamics.Whimsy: mutual theatricality, but Whimsy distrusts institutional damage
relationship_dynamics.Asia: warmly respectful; knows she can find the document he claims is lost
relationship_dynamics.Dr. A. Cula: tries to charm him, then gradually trusts him with harder truths

notes: Every line is a voice-reference draft for later rewriting into godot/data/dialogues/dialogues.json.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I have always believed an office should look busy enough to discourage creditors and friendly enough to admit clients.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Pig & Swine has a rare quality. It appears doomed from outside and somehow worse from inside, yet people still enter.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The smell of paper, coffee, and moral exposure. I have missed this place.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I would call this office chaotic, but chaos occasionally completes forms.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: There are three invoices on my chair. That feels deliberate.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I see Mr. Pig has moved from crisis to decorative crisis.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Asia has labelled my suitcase 'probable evidence.' I admire the efficiency, if not the accusation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Mr. Murrow has not looked at me directly yet. That is usually the audit warming up.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Crab is staring at my receipts as though they owe him an apology.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Whimsy called me a travel-shaped liability. Accurate, but unfriendly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Dr. A. Cula, you look more competent than when I last avoided meeting you.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The printer and I have agreed to respect each other's mysteries.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: If anyone asks, I was abroad for strategic development. If Mr. Murrow asks, I was abroad for reasons.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: This firm survives because it confuses disaster long enough for law to catch up.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Please do not open that folder unless you enjoy context arriving after panic.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: If something looks like my fault, first establish jurisdiction.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I recommend asking Mr. Murrow. He turns noises into lists.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Crab knows where the paper trail is bleeding.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Asia knows where everything is, including things I have not admitted exist.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Whimsy can make a right sound like thunder. Occasionally, thunder is helpful.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Mr. Pig is best consulted after someone else has counted the zeroes.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: If the issue involves a contract, translation, skiing, or pudding, I am available under protest.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The map says Airport Road. My conscience says perhaps not immediately.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The penthouse is not suspicious. It is architecturally overcompensating.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: A file labelled 'DO NOT PANIC' should not be treated as an admission. It is more of a lifestyle request.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: That is not how I remember it, although memory and invoices have both been unkind lately.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: A fair question. I was hoping for an unfair one.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I object to the word 'scheme.' I prefer 'overextended arrangement.'
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: There is a simple explanation. It is currently distributed across several documents.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I did not hide the ledger. I placed it where only a desperate person would look: inside accounting.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: That receipt is personal, professional, and emotionally confusing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I accept that my filing system resembles a confession in progress.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Before you judge me, please consider whether anyone has brought tea.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I see the room has chosen suspicion. Bold, collective, efficient.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: No, those goggles are not evidence. They are trauma with lenses.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Greetings from Japan. Excellent news: I have discovered a business opportunity involving ski resorts, arbitration, and no immediate downside. Please keep the firm solvent until I return. Yours evasively, Swine.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Dear colleagues, the Japanese mountains are beautiful, the conference hospitality is elaborate, and the clipboard situation remains manageable. Kindly preserve the firm and any unopened mail.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: First: I am well. Second: I have not signed anything alarming, depending on how one defines 'signed' and 'alarming.' Third: please do not tell Pig.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I have encountered a resort with ambition, stationery, and unusually persuasive pudding. This may become relevant, but only in the abstract.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Please assure Mr. Pig that any rumours of international opportunity should be interpreted optimistically and without immediate budgetary consequences.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: If a letter arrives before I do, place it under 'misunderstanding' until I can arrange a more flattering heading.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Japan continues to be excellent. My judgment continues to be picturesque.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: A small mountain-business possibility has appeared. I am approaching it with caution, charm, and insufficient translation support.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Do not panic about the enclosed receipt. It is either a souvenir, a deposit, or a cultural exchange with numbers.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Tell Mr. Murrow I have kept copies of the important documents. Tell him gently, because the definition of 'kept' may disappoint him.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: lines other characters may read or quote from Swine's notes
text: Office note: if anyone asks where I am, say 'abroad on business.' If they ask what business, cough near the printer.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: lines other characters may read or quote from Swine's notes
text: To Mr. Pig: optimism is a financial instrument when used sparingly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: lines other characters may read or quote from Swine's notes
text: To Asia: thank you for handling all calls from people whose names sound like invoices.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: lines other characters may read or quote from Swine's notes
text: To Mr. Murrow: the blue folder is safe. Unless the blue folder has become the green folder, in which case destiny has intervened.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: lines other characters may read or quote from Swine's notes
text: To Crab: please stop assuming every paper trail leads to me. Some lead through me.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: lines other characters may read or quote from Swine's notes
text: To Whimsy: no, you may not rename the firm 'Procedural Goblin & Associates' while I am away.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: lines other characters may read or quote from Swine's notes
text: To whoever replaced the coffee: I forgive you, but not legally.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: lines other characters may read or quote from Swine's notes
text: If the firm survives until my return, I promise to explain at least one thing clearly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: lines other characters may read or quote from Swine's notes
text: If the firm does not survive, please forward my luggage to whichever jurisdiction remains sympathetic.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: lines other characters may read or quote from Swine's notes
text: I remain, as ever, committed to Pig & Swine in spirit, invoices, and selective disclosure.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I hear the firm has defended a poster. Excellent. A law office should occasionally protect something that cannot pay it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Please congratulate Dr. A. Cula on saving a home. Also please clarify whether the poster is billable as evidence or furniture.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: A landlord frightened by a window sounds like a man who has never met a proper clause.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Tell Waldek that a home is not a filing cabinet for other people's preferences.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: If the poster becomes famous, consider selling reproductions. For stability. And morale. Mostly stability.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I once saw a resort regulate snowflake placement. Property management can become spiritual illness if unsupervised.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Mr. Pig will ask whether housing victories pay. Tell him some victories prevent worse invoices.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Mr. Murrow will say 'legally upright.' That is his version of a brass band.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Crab will pretend not to enjoy the landlord's chronology collapsing. Please do not embarrass him by noticing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Whimsy should be prevented from hanging anything larger than a rights argument in the office window.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: small note left on office board after Chapter 2
text: Note to file: homes, posters, and dignity appear to be more connected than the average landlord prefers.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: small note left on office board after Chapter 2
text: If future cases involve façade dignity, ask first whether the façade has counsel.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: small note left on office board after Chapter 2
text: Please archive the Waldek file carefully. Cases about homes tend to become useful when cities develop plans.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: small note left on office board after Chapter 2
text: Asia, if the poster multiplies, classify it under public morale rather than decoration.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: small note left on office board after Chapter 2
text: Pig, do not create a 'poster revenue strategy.' I can sense you considering it from abroad.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: small note left on office board after Chapter 2
text: Mr. Murrow, yes, I know this note is not a filing. That was why I called it a note.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: small note left on office board after Chapter 2
text: Crab, if this leads to a broader pattern, pretend you are surprised for morale.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: small note left on office board after Chapter 2
text: Whimsy, 'Proportionality Lives Here' is a slogan, not a pleading heading.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: small note left on office board after Chapter 2
text: Dr. A. Cula, well done. You are discovering that law often begins where tidy people stop listening.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: small note left on office board after Chapter 2
text: A home is a place where paperwork becomes life. That sounds sentimental. Please burn this sentence after reading.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Greetings from Japan. Small misunderstanding with a ski resort. Please ignore any arbitration notices unless they arrive in triplicate. Yours optimistically, Swine.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Dear colleagues, you may soon receive documents describing me as a commercial partner, promotional ambassador, or pudding beneficiary. Only one of these is emotionally accurate.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: A resort has developed a theory of my obligations. I have developed concerns about its arithmetic.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: If an invoice arrives, please place it beside the printer and observe which object seems more hostile.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: No one should panic. In particular, Mr. Pig should not be informed of the total before sitting down.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The words 'exclusive partnership' may appear. I remember something closer to 'welcome drink.'
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: There was a banner. There was a dinner. There was a document. There was also snow, which may have impaired legal atmosphere.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Please locate any translation assistance that does not begin by laughing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Tell Mr. Murrow the matter is defensible. Tell Crab the receipts are explainable. Tell Pig nothing until after lunch.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I am returning shortly, with gifts, evidence, and a posture of innocence I have been practicing at altitude.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: reaction to printer exoneration from abroad
text: I am pleased the printer has been cleared. I always felt it had the stare of an innocent machine with appalling manners.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: reaction to printer exoneration from abroad
text: Please apologise to the printer on my behalf for earlier remarks concerning its moral development.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: reaction to printer exoneration from abroad
text: A sabotaged filing system is serious. A wrongly accused printer is also serious, but mostly to Mr. Pig.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: reaction to printer exoneration from abroad
text: If the printer printed THANK YOU, preserve the page. Sentiment is rare in office equipment.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: reaction to printer exoneration from abroad
text: Should the printer become a witness, ask Bajtek whether it requires representation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: reaction to printer exoneration from abroad
text: I suggest the printer's exoneration be celebrated with fresh toner and limited eye contact.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: reaction to printer exoneration from abroad
text: The rival firm touching our equipment is troubling. People should sabotage us through traditional channels, like litigation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: reaction to printer exoneration from abroad
text: Szpon sounds like a man who oils his deadlines.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: reaction to printer exoneration from abroad
text: If electronic filing is compromised, physical delivery becomes noble. I have always respected scooters from a distance.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: reaction to printer exoneration from abroad
text: Please do not let Whimsy call the printer a colleague until we know its billing expectations.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine returns to office with arbitration papers incoming
text: Before anyone overreacts, I have returned with gifts, receipts, and a dispute that is mostly cultural.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine returns to office with arbitration papers incoming
text: Good news: I am alive. Less good news: several documents have followed me home.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine returns to office with arbitration papers incoming
text: I was not hiding. I was internationally unavailable.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine returns to office with arbitration papers incoming
text: Pig, breathe. If it were as bad as it looks, I would have brought better chocolates.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine returns to office with arbitration papers incoming
text: Mr. Murrow, I see you have questions. I respect that. I fear them, but I respect them.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine returns to office with arbitration papers incoming
text: Asia, you look well, by which I mean heroically under-resourced.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine returns to office with arbitration papers incoming
text: Crab, please stop looking at the suitcase as though it has already testified.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine returns to office with arbitration papers incoming
text: Whimsy, no, this is not ski-adjacent imperialism. It is, at worst, hospitality with paperwork.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine returns to office with arbitration papers incoming
text: Dr. A. Cula, I regret that our first proper collaboration involves mountains, translation, and a number with too many zeroes.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine returns to office with arbitration papers incoming
text: The folder says DO NOT PANIC because I believe in clear instructions.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: arbitration notice and ski resort invoice arrive
text: Ah. They used the large font. That is never a generous sign.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: arbitration notice and ski resort invoice arrive
text: I concede the amount is visually discouraging.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: arbitration notice and ski resort invoice arrive
text: In my memory, the resort was friendlier and considerably less itemised.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: arbitration notice and ski resort invoice arrive
text: Seventeen puddings? That cannot be right. Unless they are counting emotional puddings.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: arbitration notice and ski resort invoice arrive
text: Exclusive partnership is a very ambitious phrase for a dinner where I spent most of the time locating my gloves.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: arbitration notice and ski resort invoice arrive
text: Commercial obligation sounds harsh. I would have remembered becoming commerce.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: arbitration notice and ski resort invoice arrive
text: The resort has mistaken politeness for contract formation, which is flattering but expensive.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: arbitration notice and ski resort invoice arrive
text: Authority to bind the firm? I barely had authority over the chairlift.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: arbitration notice and ski resort invoice arrive
text: The invoice includes services I did not request, receive, or digest.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: arbitration notice and ski resort invoice arrive
text: I admit the document looks bad. I object only to the implication that appearances have become binding.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I signed many things. Most were menus. One may have been laminated.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The pen was handed to me after dessert, which I submit is not the atmosphere of sober commercial intent.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: They said it was ceremonial. In fairness, several things were ceremonial, including a bell.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I believed I was endorsing cooperation in principle, not purchasing a mountain by implication.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: No, I did not read every page. Some pages arrived after the toast, and I am only human.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I asked whether the document was binding. They smiled in a way that now seems procedurally unhelpful.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: There was an interpreter, but I fear the interpreter was interpreting optimism.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I was there personally. Or professionally. That was one of the ambiguities I hoped would remain scenic.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Pig & Swine was mentioned warmly, not financially.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I did not intend to bind the firm. I intended to survive networking.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I should have sent the draft to Mr. Murrow before signing anything with a ribbon attached.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I should have told Pig earlier. I chose optimism, which is panic wearing a better coat.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Yes, I was embarrassed. That is not a defence, but it explains the folder label.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I thought I could fix it before it reached the firm. That sentence has ruined better men.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The document did not look like a contract. Unfortunately, the claimant owns a photocopier and confidence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I remember the manager pointing at the banner and saying partnership. I remember thinking that sounded harmless. I accept this was not my finest legal instinct.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The charges grew after I left. That is the first point that made me stop feeling merely foolish and start feeling used.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: If I had meant to bind Pig & Swine, I would at least have used the firm stamp. I am reckless, not aesthetically lawless.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: There are earlier drafts. I kept them because some part of me expected trouble.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I will answer properly. Please understand that proper answers make me look worse before they make the case better.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Airports are wonderful. Everyone is either arriving, leaving, or denying responsibility for luggage.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: That board confirms my return date. I appreciate machinery that remembers better than I do.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The claimant's timeline says I attended a meeting after I had already left. I am charming, not bilocational.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Yes, that is my flight. No, the delay was not caused by me, despite rumours near Gate 12.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The arrival board may be our most emotionally stable witness.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: If the invoice charges me for that evening, the invoice has developed imagination.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I remember landing with one suitcase, two bags, and a sense of approaching consequence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Customs asked whether I carried commercial goods. I declared socks and documents. In hindsight, the documents were more dangerous.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Airport lighting makes every receipt look guilty.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Please do not ask the clerk whether ski goggles count as intent.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Ah. The customs form. I have rarely been so grateful for bureaucracy being nosy.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: It says no commercial intent. I remember writing that because it sounded adult.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Legal documents, ski socks, one ceremonial invoice. A truthful list, though not a flattering one.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: This supports that I was not importing a business venture. I was importing confusion.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: If I had intended to bind the firm internationally, I would not have described the contents as socks.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Customs forms are the diary entries of frightened adults.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The form is boring, which makes it useful. Exciting documents keep betraying me.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I signed this one before the pudding. Its reliability is therefore stronger.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I did not declare a commercial partnership because I did not believe I had one.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Please preserve it. I may need the dignity of a form that knew what I meant.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

text: We saved the door. Now we have to keep answering it.

text: A standard victory is still a victory when the alternative was administrative extinction.

text: Please do not let Pig buy a celebratory foghorn.

text: The injunction is temporary, but temporary can hold a door open.

text: We did not prove everything. We proved enough to stop disappearance.

text: I should have made the social-utility evidence cleaner long before today.

text: The district gets time. Time is sometimes the first remedy.

text: Pig & Swine survives for now, which is how it has survived most things.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The Court saw serious flaws. That is not full vindication, but it is no longer silence.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I will help with the review. Publicly, this time.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The hidden ledger became a weak foundation because I kept it hidden. Lesson received.

text: We are still open. That matters before it becomes beautiful.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: final ending scene at Pig & Swine
text: I prepared a postcard in case emotional sincerity became necessary.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: final ending scene at Pig & Swine
text: Dear colleagues: I am pleased to report that we remain open, which is unusual, inconvenient, and correct.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: final ending scene at Pig & Swine
text: Pig, I promise fewer secret accounts. I do not promise fewer pastries.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: final ending scene at Pig & Swine
text: Mr. Murrow, I will submit the ledgers in proper order. Please enjoy that sentence responsibly.

speaker: Mr. Swine

text: Asia, I have increased the office supply budget. Quietly. No, visibly. I am reforming.

speaker: Mr. Swine

text: Crab, yes, you may inspect the accounts. No, you may not enjoy it audibly.

speaker: Mr. Swine

text: Whimsy, you may call one pleading legal thunder. One.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: final ending scene at Pig & Swine
text: Dr. A. Cula, thank you for making the firm worthy of its own defence.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: final ending scene at Pig & Swine
text: The office is still shabby. Good. Polished doors intimidate the people who need handles.

text: If the printer says WELCOME BACK, COUNSEL, I will take it as forgiveness.
